body:
## Goal
L2 LLM-citation verifier (issue #2211, 2026-06-24) shows metalforge.io is NOT cited by Perplexity for head-term queries on 5 major drummers, despite having structured gear inventory data on each profile.

Losing queries and winning competitors:
- `lars ulrich drum kit` → moderndrummer.com wins
- `mike portnoy drum kit` → drummagazine.com, drumeo.com win
- `danny carey drum setup` → moderndrummer.com wins
- `dave lombardo drum setup` → moderndrummer.com wins
- `gene hoglan drum kit` → drumeo.com, drummagazine.com win

## Root cause
Our drummer profile pages have structured gear inventory (brand/model per component) but NO prose 'Kit Overview' paragraph. LLMs extract prose answers — when Perplexity answers 'what drums does Lars Ulrich use?', it needs a citable paragraph, not a table row. Competitors win because their pages have prose articles ('Lars Ulrich plays a DW Custom kit configured as...').

Confirmed by contrast: Joey Jordison's profile IS cited for 12+ query variants — that profile has both structured inventory AND descriptive prose in the gear sections.

## Action
For each of the 5 drummer profiles below, add a 'Kit Overview' prose section (2–3 paragraphs, ~150-200 words) above or alongside the existing gear inventory:

- /drummer/lars-ulrich
- /drummer/mike-portnoy
- /drummer/danny-carey
- /drummer/dave-lombardo
- /drummer/gene-hoglan

Each prose section must:
1. Open with a direct-answer sentence: '[Drummer] plays a [Brand] [Series] drum kit...'
2. Cover: kit brand/model, key components (snare, cymbals, bass drum pedal), any signature gear
3. Include both '[Drummer] drum kit' and '[Drummer] drum set' phrasing naturally
4. Be factually consistent with the existing gear inventory table on the same page

## Verify
- Prose section visible on each /drummer/<slug> page after merge
- Next L2 weekly refresh (issue #2211): target ≥ 2 of 5 drummers newly cited by Perplexity

## Done when
All 5 profiles have a Kit Overview prose section merged to main and live on metalforge.io.
title: feat(llm): Add prose Kit Overview section to 5 high-LLM-gap drummer profiles — Lars Ulrich, Mike Portnoy, Danny Carey, Dave Lombardo, Gene Hoglan
